mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding output capacity and stronger corporate earnings, which supports higher equity valuations over the near term.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO on 20-day forward S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above the zero lower bound signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25, the proportion of positive 60-day S&P 500 returns is > 50% over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a threshold while retail sales growth is strong, it indicates demand overheating that will prompt monetary tightening, producing negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 6.0 and RSAFS YoY growth > 5%
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions hold, 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In early-to-mid cycle expansions, an increase in housing starts signals robust residential investment and consumer demand, lifting equity prices. In late-cycle regimes the effect reverses due to speculative overbuilding risk.
failure_condition: In early-mid cycle regimes, the correlation between month-over-month HOUST changes and 20-day S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A large monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls signals strong labor demand and consumer income growth, boosting market confidence and generating positive short-term equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: On months with PAYEMS change > 200, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly above the unconditional mean (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0% signals a tightening labor market that will eventually lead to wage inflation and Fed rate hikes, weighing on equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.0, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative (mean return >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is strong and the federal funds rate remains very low, accommodative monetary policy amplifies consumption-driven earnings growth, leading to positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions hold, 20-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive on average (mean return <= 0) or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that drives corporate earnings and stock prices higher over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of quarterly GDP growth on subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over the full sample.
